primary_sources.title: Second Malaysia Plan 1971-1975 (Economic Planning Unit, Prime Minister's Department)
Constitution (Amendment) Act 1971 (Act A30) — Article 153 strengthening; Sedition (Amendment) Act 1971

policy_content_summary: New Economic Policy 1971-1990: two-pronged programme of poverty eradication regardless of race, and economic restructuring to reduce identification of race with economic function. Operationalises the constitutional 'special position' (Article 153) into administrative practice: 30% Bumiputera corporate-equity target by 1990; Bumiputera quotas in education, housing, business licences, federal procurement.
notes: Operationalises the 'Bumiputera' institutional category. Sets the 30% corporate-equity target for 1990, never met (estimates 18-23% by 1990 depending on methodology). NEP succeeded by NDP (1990), NVP (2001), NEM (2010), Bumi Economic Transformation Roadmap, PuTERA35. The 1971 launch is the structural moment where ethnic-restructuring becomes the dominant policy frame of the Malaysian state for the following five decades. Constitutional amendments accompanying NEP further entrenched Malay-rulers-Islam position (Article 153 reinforced, Sedition Act expanded to cover questioning of Articles 152/153/181).
